interview_summary:
KEY FINDINGS
============================================================

1. RETRIEVAL IS THE BOTTLENECK
   52% of all examples fail at retrieval — the correct
   chunk never makes it into the top-5 results.
   Fix this first: better chunking, domain embeddings, query expansion.

2. MODELS ARE EQUIVALENT ON RAG
   All three models (LoRA, Full SFT, GRPO) show nearly identical
   error distributions. The training method doesn't matter when
   retrieval fails — you can't reason over missing context.

3. GRPO'S REAL IMPACT IS ON REASONING
   With gold context, GRPO hits 68% vs ~40% for SFT.
   The model learned better financial reasoning, but RAG
   caps the end-to-end system at 17%.

4. WRONG EXTRACTION (24%) IS THE #2 PROBLEM
   Model finds the right document but pulls numbers from the
   wrong row/column. This is a table-understanding problem —
   better table serialization or structured extraction would help.

5. PRODUCTION PRIORITY ORDER
   1. Improve retrieval (domain embeddings, better chunking)
   2. Better table parsing (structured extraction)
   3. More GRPO training data (expand beyond FinQA)

INTERVIEW SOUND BITE
------------------------------------------------------------
"52% of errors are retrieval misses — the model never sees the
right context. 17% are wrong number extraction from tables.
11% are wrong math operations. So the #1 lever is retrieval,
not more training. GRPO proves this: 68% with gold context,
17% with RAG — the model can reason, it just needs the right data."
